title: Session display Phase 3 â€” Detach (detachable windows)
body:
## Requirement

**Session display Phase 3 â€” Detach: detachable session windows.** Move a session into its own OS window (draggable to a 2nd monitor), session keeps running; reattach back into the main window.

- **Legacy stays the default** (non-breaking); detach is an **action**, not a third exclusive mode. Controlled via setting `global.sessionDisplayMode`.
- Builds on **Phase 1 tabs (#6)** + **Phase 2 settings window (#7)** (both done) â€” uses the multi-window pattern proven there.
- Central change: route `terminal-data` per session to the owning window (sidebar/attention events stay in the main window).

Detailed design and implementation plan â†’ comment.

title: Terminal: working-status line renders stacked/duplicated (rare) â€” distinct from #85 garble
body:
## Requirement

The Claude working-status line occasionally renders **stacked/duplicated** â€” several
successive status frames sit on top of each other (e.g. three `Hashingâ€¦` lines with
*different* content: `22 24`, `16s â†“401`, `11s â†“138 Â· thought for 5s`) instead of the
single line overwriting in place. A resize heals it. **Occurs rarely.** Seen on build
`main @ 7e473e7` (which already carries the DPR-refit fix), WebGL off (DOM renderer).

This is **distinct from #85** (that was the horizontal char-drift *garble* = xterm.js#6015,
addressed by the DPR-refit). This is **vertical duplication** â€” old status rows not cleared
when the TUI rewrites the status line â€” a different mechanism.

**Candidate:** xterm.js#5801 (OPEN) â€” "ED2 (`\x1b[2J`) inside a DEC 2026 synchronized update
block resets viewportY, causing scroll yank during streaming redraws." Matches stacked status
frames during sync redraws. To be confirmed.

**Expected:** a single, in-place-updating status line; no stacking.

## Diagnosis (in progress)

Temporary probe added on a debug branch (never merged): **F5** = pure `terminal.refresh(0, rows-1)`
(render repaint), **F8** = `safeFit` (geometry re-fit) on the focused session. Press each on the
stacked ghost:
- F5 clears it â†’ dirty-row render-ghost â†’ a refresh-heal fixes it.
- F5 fails, F8/resize clears it â†’ geometry/re-fit class.
- Neither â†’ buffer/sync-viewport (xterm.js#5801) â†’ upstream / deeper.

Keep open until the mechanism is identified.

title: WebGL context loss leaves a stale fit (no re-fit, no repaint)
body:
`WebglAddon.onContextLoss` (`public/terminal-manager.js`) disposes the addon and marks the DOM renderer as suggested, but never re-fits the terminal.

xterm silently continues on its DOM renderer, whose cell metrics differ from WebGL's (xterm.js#6015). Every other renderer switch compensates: `loadTerminalWebgl()` calls `safeFit()` after loading, and `showSession()` re-fits on the next frame when it restores a context suspended by the grid's off-screen handling. The loss path does neither.

Result: a visible terminal that loses its GL context keeps a fit computed for WebGL cell metrics while rendering with DOM metrics â€” the bottom row can clip, the same failure mode #81 traced to renderer swaps under fractional display scaling. It self-heals only on the next tab switch, when `showSession()` reloads WebGL and re-fits.

Raising the GL-context budget to 32 narrows the window but does not close it: driver-side losses still occur.

### Acceptance
- `onContextLoss` re-fits the terminal and forces a full repaint.
- The re-fit is deferred by one frame â€” cell metrics are reported only after the renderer swap has painted, matching the existing restore path.
- No re-fit is attempted on a terminal destroyed between the loss and the frame.

Split out of #81 (found while re-reviewing its WebGL invariants).

title: Settings: export & import (global settings, JSON file)
body:
Let users export their global settings to a JSON file and import them back â€” for backup and moving a configuration to another machine.

## Requirement

- **Export**: write the global settings blob to a user-chosen `.json` file via a native save dialog. Envelope: `{ version, exportedAt, global: {â€¦} }`.
- **Import**: pick a `.json` file via a native open dialog, validate it, and **merge** its `global` values onto the existing global blob (read-merge-write; unknown/future keys preserved). Applied live (settings + affected UI refresh), no restart.
- **Never export/import**: the `db_version` marker, or `saved_variables` (encrypted, OS-bound secrets â€” separate table). Settings blobs contain no secrets today.
- Scope v1: **global blob only** â€” no `project:<path>` blobs (they embed absolute local paths â†’ not portable, PII).
- Entry point: Export/Import buttons in the Settings viewer.
- UI text English.

Note: if multi-LLM (#141) later lands credentials into the settings blob, export redaction must be revisited.

Plan/design in comments.

title: Onboarding: first-time-launch welcome screen
body:
Show a welcome screen on the very first launch to orient a new user, then never again unless reset.

## Requirement

- On first launch (fresh install â€” no global settings yet), show a welcome overlay: brief intro to the app + primary actions.
- **Actions**: "Import settings" (ties into settings import â€” migrate from another machine) and "Open settings" (first-time configuration), plus a Dismiss/close.
- Dismissing persists a flag so it never shows again (store `welcomeDismissed` in the global settings blob via `merge-setting`, so it rides existing persistence + export/import).
- Style matches existing dialogs (`new-session-overlay` pattern). UI text English.

Depends on the settings export/import feature for the "Import settings" action.

Plan/design in comments.

title: Real git-worktree awareness â€” treat `git worktree add` worktrees as first-class projects
body:
## Requirement

Switchboard only recognizes **convention-based** worktrees (`.claude/worktrees/â€¦`, `.worktrees/â€¦`) â€” it
collapses those to their parent project. A **real git worktree** (created via `git worktree add`, i.e. a
`.git` **file** pointing at `<mainrepo>/.git/worktrees/<name>`, sharing the main repo's `--git-common-dir`
but with its own `--show-toplevel`) is **not** handled: its sessions get attributed to the **parent repo
project** and the worktree cannot be added/kept as its own live project.

**Requirement:** detect real git worktrees and treat each as a **first-class project** â€” addable, listed,
and grouped on its own top-level path, **not** collapsed into the parent repo. Grouping must key on the
worktree's actual working tree, not on the shared git dir.

**Also:** a session whose working directory **changes mid-session** (started in the parent repo, then
moved into a worktree â€” its JSONL then carries two `cwd` values) is currently attributed by the **first**
`cwd` line, so it lands under the parent. Project attribution should follow the session's **actual /
current** working tree, not just the head line.

### Notes / findings (design goes in comments)
- `derive-project-path.js` `resolveWorktreePath()` â€” pattern-matches only the `.claude/worktrees` convention;
  no real-git-worktree detection.
- `main.js` `WORKTREE_PATH_RE` + `delete-worktree`/`worktree-status` â€” convention-based only.
- `add-project` (`main.js`) â€” no git-worktree awareness.
- `session-cache.js` `buildProjectsFromCache()` â€” groups by exact `projectPath`; no worktree/git-root logic.
- `derive-project-path.js` `deriveProjectPath()` â€” reads `cwd` from the JSONL **head** lines (â†’ the
  mid-session-move attribution bug).

### Relation
Adjacent to the multi-LLM rework's project-grouping layer (that effort rewrites `derive-project-path` /
`scan-projects` and makes cwd-grouping central). Best implemented **there** so the new grouping is
worktree-aware from the start; cross-referenced in that plan.

title: Remote repo over SSH (treat like local)
body:
## Requirement

**Remote repo over SSH (treat like local)** _(Source: supacode)_ â€” open a repo/project on a remote host via SSH and manage it in Switchboard like a local one: session scan, terminal (PTY over SSH), file/diff access. **Large** â€” needs its own detailed plan (transport, auth via Bitwarden SSH agent, remote `~/.claude/projects` scan, PTY bridging, watcher replacement). Clarify scope/architecture before implementation.

title: Detect subagent spawn via .meta.json (not only .jsonl)
body:
## Requirement

**Detect subagent spawn via `.meta.json` (not only `.jsonl`)** â€” when spawning a task subagent, Claude Code immediately writes `<parent>/subagents/agent-<id>.meta.json` (`agentType`, `description`, `toolUseId`, `spawnDepth`), but the `.jsonl` transcript only once the agent produces output. A spawned agent that did not (yet) run to producing output (queued, parent turn ends/is interrupted) has **only** `meta.json` â†’ `detectSubagentTransitions`/`enumerateSessionFiles` (which only scan `subagents/*.jsonl`) never see it (observed 2026-07-02: session `876c2b28`, "Feldtexte W6" agents â€” meta identical to runnable W5 agents, but without jsonl; parent last at 22:26, spawns at 22:28). **No special background-agents format, no data loss â€” pure visibility.** Fix: also scan `subagents/*.meta.json` â†’ placeholder subagent row (name from `description`, status "spawned/empty/aborted") as soon as the meta is there; upgrade to a full row when the `.jsonl` arrives. Files: `read-session-file.js` (`enumerateSessionFiles`), `session-transitions.js`, `session-cache.js`, subagent rendering in `public/sidebar.js`.

title: Per-session API key override
body:
## Requirement

**Per-session API key override** _(split off from #47)_ â€” optional password field in the session dialogs that injects `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` **only transiently** per session into the PTY `env` (`open-terminal`, `main.js`) â€” **never** in the command string or the settings DB. Persistence only via `saved_variables` `secret` (`safeStorage`). Picker layer from `brianstanley` @ `fb81d18`. **Net-new** (we are OAuth/Claude-only) â€” before implementing, clarify whether it's even wanted.

title: Make the terminal LRU cap configurable
body:
The renderer keeps at most 12 live xterm instances (`TERMINAL_LRU_CAP` in `public/terminal-manager.js`); the least-recently-shown closed session beyond the cap is destroyed (soft cap: running and active sessions are never evicted).

Since WebGL is the default renderer, the cap also serves as the GL-context budget (Chromium allows ~16 per process; a lost context falls back to the DOM renderer).

Expose the cap as a setting for users with many parallel sessions and enough RAM. Constraints: keep 12 as the default; values above ~14 should hint that terminals beyond the GL budget silently fall back to the DOM renderer.

Deferred from #81 (was rejected there as "defer until the cap is hit").

title: Session tags: add a discoverable entry point (can't add the first tag)
body:
## Problem

Session tags exist (display chips via `decorateSessionItem`, editor via `editTags` in `bookmarks-tags.js`) but are effectively unusable: the **only** way to open the tag editor is clicking an **existing** tag chip. A session with no tags shows no chip, so there is **no way to add the first tag** from the UI â€” no button, no context-menu entry.

## Requirement

Add a discoverable entry point to edit a session's tags, e.g. an "Edit tagsâ€¦" item in the session right-click/context menu (and/or a small add-tag affordance). Opening it should call the existing `editTags(session)` dialog.

title: Evaluate: multi-sidebar panes
body: Evaluate porting aaaron `feat/multi-sidebar-panes` â€” multiple sidebar panes. Source: aaaronmiller/switchboard @ feat/multi-sidebar-panes.

title: Evaluate: local Whisper voice input
body: Evaluate porting ivandobsky `feat/local-whisper-voice` â€” local Whisper voice control/input. Source: ivandobskygithub/switchboard @ feat/local-whisper-voice.

title: Evaluate: brianstanley cursor-ui-refresh
body: Evaluate brianstanley `cursor-ui-refresh` â€” UI refresh. Source: brianstanley/switchboard @ cursor-ui-refresh. We already ported brianstanley's Variables + preview panel; check what's new/portable.

title: Optional second tray icon showing Claude usage (live percentage)
body:
Add an optional **second system-tray icon** that shows current Claude usage, enabled via Settings (off by default).

## Requirement
- A live-rendered tray icon showing the current usage percentage, styled like a usage monitor: a **colored progress ring** or a **badge** with the number baked into the icon, color-coded by the existing warn/critical thresholds (green â†’ amber â†’ red).
- **Tooltip** with the details we already show in the status bar (5h / 7d, extra usage, reset time).
- Small **context menu**: show/hide the window, open Stats, refresh, quit.
- **Cross-platform**: macOS, Windows, Linux.
- **Reuse the usage data the app already has** â€” the same values the status bar renders. **No separate polling loop / no extra network fetch** for the tray; it updates whenever the existing status-bar usage updates.
- Enabled + styled via a **Settings** toggle (icon style ring/badge). When off, the second tray is not created; toggling off destroys it.

Reference behaviour: a small pystray + Pillow usage-monitor tray that draws a ring/badge with the percentage and threshold colors.

title: Terminal: evaluate VSCode-style shell integration (OSC 633) for plain terminals
body:
Idea from the VSCode draw-path comparison (#87/#114 context). VSCode's shell integration injects a shell profile hook that emits OSC 633 sequences (command start / command end / exit code / cwd), enabling command tracking, "run recent command", per-command scrollback navigation, and precise prompt detection.

**Requirement:** evaluate whether a slim variant is worth it for our **plain terminals** (not Claude sessions â€” the CLI is a fullscreen TUI where command tracking is meaningless): e.g. exit-code badges, jump-to-previous-command in scrollback, or more reliable "shell is idle" detection. Likely low value for this app's core use case; document a decision either way. Large feature â€” only pursue with a concrete user-facing benefit.

title: Persist incremental parse state so cold-start scans read only the delta
Multi-LLM system: profiles (alt endpoints) + runtimes (Codex/Copilot/â€¦)
body:
## Requirement

Umbrella issue for turning Switchboard into a **multi-LLM system**. This is a large, multi-phase
effort; the detailed step-by-step plan lives **locally, out of git** (own branch + `docs/plans/`).
This issue holds only the **target state** ("what it should look like"), not the implementation steps.

Consolidates the previously-separate multi-LLM issues: **#42** (runtimes), **#141** (profiles),
**#103** (agent teams), **#41** (agent badges), **#106** (analytics). Those are closed and folded here.

---

### Axis A â€” LLM profiles (same binary, alternative endpoint) â€” from #141

Run the *same* Claude Code CLI against an Anthropic-compatible endpoint (DeepSeek, GLM/Z.ai,
OpenRouter, or a user's own) by merging a per-session **environment-variable bundle** at spawn.
Smaller, self-contained, no runtime rework.

- **Profile** = user record `{ id, name, env: {â€¦}, icon }`. `env` holds `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`, model
  names, and an auth **reference** (`$VAR`, resolved against host env at spawn â€” never a literal
  secret on disk; repo is public).
- **Spawn**: merge resolved profile env into the PTY env (profile overrides base). New + resume;
  resume keeps the recorded profile, with an override picker.
- **Mapping**: `sessionId â†’ profileId` persisted (own JSON file, not the session_cache DB; tempâ†’real
  session-id rekey on transition), exposed to the renderer for restart-surviving badges.
- **No new scanner / directory / watcher** â€” sessions still write to `~/.claude/projects/**`.
- **UI**: profile-management modal (create/edit/delete, presets, icon, default); launch/resume
  popover lists profiles; sidebar monogram badges (badge every session only in "mixed mode" â€” â‰¥2
  backends live).
- **Presets** to seed the editor: Anthropic (default), DeepSeek, GLM (Z.ai), OpenRouter. Cap profile
  count and env-var count; store `$VAR` references only.
- **DeepSeek quirk** (ivandobsky `7e0d1e8`): DeepSeek validates `user_id` against `^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$`;
  Claude Code's default hashed+subagent-suffixed id (colons/dots) triggers mid-session 400s once
  subagents spawn â€” needs a `CLAUDE_CODE_EXTRA_BODY` metadata override in the DeepSeek preset.
- Reference: ivandobsky `main` â€” `profiles.js`, `session-profiles.js`,
  `public/{profile-presets,profiles-panel,profile-icons}.js`, spawn env merge in `main.js`. Template,
  not a cherry-pick.

### Axis B â€” Multi-runtime (different agent binary) â€” from #42

Alongside Claude Code, launch & manage other coding CLIs as a session type: **Codex**, GitHub Copilot
CLI, OpenCode, and others. Affects session start (command/args per agent), detection/scan (not just
`~/.claude/projects`), possibly a dedicated session-cache source. Large; needs the runtime rework
Axis A avoids.

### Open design questions (were evaluate-spikes)

- **Agent teams / multi-agent orchestration** (#103): two forks have it â€” kreaddis
  `agent-teams-proto` and ivandobsky `feat/agent-teams`. Compare both, decide scope.
- **Agent badges â€” which tool runs where** (#41, source supacode): per session/row a badge showing
  the agent type (Claude / Codex / Copilot / OpenCode) + live presence, hook-driven. Partial overlap
  with #13 (status chips, Claude only). Decide: standalone vs folded into #13. Only meaningful with
  Axis B.

### Analytics hook-up â€” from #106

`backendId â‰¡ profileId`. The `session_metrics` table (per session/date/model) already supports a
per-profile/per-model breakdown via a join once the `sessionId â†’ profile` map (Axis A) exists.

---

### Notes

- **Secrets**: auth as `$VAR` references, resolved at spawn, unresolved refs dropped â€” never write
  literal keys to disk (public repo + secret-hygiene rules).
- **Sequencing**: Axis A is independent of Axis B and ships first (days vs weeks). Badges/teams depend
  on Axis B.
- Detailed plans, task lists, and design docs are kept local (own branch, `docs/plans/`, gitignored) â€”
  this issue tracks only the target state.

title: i18n: renderer localisation support layer (language-neutral, EN master)
body:
Add a language-neutral localisation **support layer** to the renderer, so any user-facing UI string can be translated. This issue is about the **infrastructure**, not the translations themselves â€” English stays the master/source of truth; concrete languages are added on top afterwards.

## Requirement (the support layer)

- A global `t('area.key', { placeholders })` available to every renderer script â€” no build step, no module system (matches our vanilla `<script>` setup).
- **English is the master.** Missing keys fall back to English, then to the key itself â€” the UI is never blank.
- Static HTML localised via `data-i18n` / `data-i18n-title` / `data-i18n-placeholder` / `data-i18n-aria` attributes, filled on load.
- Active language is a global setting (Settings UI). Changing it persists and re-renders every view in the new language.
- Extract the existing English UI strings (sidebar tabs/chrome, session filters, settings labels/dialogs/tooltips) into the dictionary, keyed. No behaviour change while only EN exists.
- Language-neutral by design: adding a language = adding one dictionary, no code changes.

## Rollout (separate follow-up issues, not this one)

Language order once the layer lands: **EN (master) â†’ DE â†’ FR**. Each added language is its own translation task on top of this support layer.

## Work organisation

Large, cross-cutting change (touches nearly every renderer file that emits text) â†’ **own feature branch**.

Reference implementation for the layer: kreaddis `public/i18n.js` (~488 lines) â€” adapt the mechanism, ignore its specific fr strings. Upstream commit `6e4d127` (fr apostrophe fix) is only relevant once translations exist.

Plan/design + string-extraction strategy go in comments.
